relations.note: Charts/tables carve out an exception to the general asymmetric accent-shape rule, replacing it with uniform corner radii.

quotes: Approved radius pairs (print): **1.25" & 0.0625"** (large containers/photos), **0.5" & 0.0625"** (banners), **0.4375" & 0.0625"** (small callout boxes).
A rectangle with **selectively enlarged opposite corner radii**; the other two corners keep a slight radius.
Be consistent within an email: same diagonal orientation across all sections (default: large radius top-left & bottom-right).

rules.intent: Preserve the signature asymmetric accent-shape silhouette consistently across containers and channels.
rules.rule_text: The core brand container device is a rectangle with selectively enlarged radii on one diagonal pair of corners (the other diagonal pair keeps a slight radius); it houses callout text, banners, and photography and reads modern/optimistic. Radius pairs are locked per size class: large containers/photos (120px & 6px in email / 1.25" & 0.0625" in print), banners (48px & 6px email / 0.5" & 0.0625" print), small callout boxes (42px & 6px email / 0.4375" & 0.0625" print). The large radius is applied to one diagonal pair (e.g. top-left + bottom-right) and the slight radius to the other; the same diagonal orientation must be used consistently across all sections within a given email, defaulting to large radius on top-left & bottom-right.

quotes: **Exception: charts and tables use uniform rounded corners on all four corners** (not the asymmetric accent shape).

rules.intent: Keep data visualizations legible and neutral by excluding them from the decorative asymmetric container device.
rules.rule_text: Charts and tables are exempt from the asymmetric brand accent shape; they instead use uniform rounded corners on all four corners at 12px (chart.container.radius = 12px), keeping data displays neutral rather than decorative.
